header:
Warnings:
  • The number of fused uops of the instruction [LEA 0x8(%RAX),%RDX] is unknown
  • The number of fused uops of the instruction [LEA 0x38(%RDX),%RAX] is unknown
4% of peak computational performance is used (1.17 out of 24.00 FLOP per cycle (GFLOPS @ 1GHz))

gain.workaround:
• Try another compiler or update/tune your current one
  • Remove inter-iterations dependences from your loop and make it unit-stride:
    • If your arrays have 2 or more dimensions, check whether elements are accessed contiguously and, otherwise, try to permute loops accordingly: Fortran storage order is column-major: do i do j a(i,j) = b(i,j) (slow, non stride 1) => do i do j a(j,i) = b(i,j) (fast, stride 1)<>
    • If your loop streams arrays of structures (AoS), try to use structures of arrays instead (SoA): do i a(i)%x = b(i)%x (slow, non stride 1) => do i a%x(i) = b%x(i) (fast, stride 1)
gain.details:
Store and arithmetical SSE/AVX instructions are used in scalar version (process only one data element in vector registers).
Since your execution units are vector units, only a vectorized loop can use their full power.
gain.title: Vectorization
gain.txt:
Your loop is probably not vectorized.
Only 28% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions).
By vectorizing your loop, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 13.67 to 3.42 cycles (4.00x speedup).

gain.workaround: Reduce the number of FP multiply/FMA instructions
gain.title: Execution units bottlenecks
gain.txt: Performance is limited by execution of FP multiply or FMA (fused multiply-add) operations (the FP multiply/FMA unit is a bottleneck).

potential.workaround:
Try to change order in which elements are evaluated (using parentheses) in arithmetic expressions containing both ADD/SUB and MUL operations to enable your compiler to generate FMA instructions wherever possible.
Estimated speedup by perfect pairing: 1.24x.
For instance a + b*c is a valid FMA (MUL then ADD).
However (a+b)* c cannot be translated into an FMA (ADD then MUL).
potential.title: FMA
potential.txt: Presence of both ADD/SUB and MUL operations.

header:
The loop is defined in /home/lbw/architecture-opt-hpc-NPBMG/NPBx/OFFICIAL-NASA/NPB3.4.4/NPB3.4-MPI/MG/mg.f90:1078-1081. The related source loop is not unrolled or unrolled with no peel/tail loop. Warnings:
  • Ignoring paths for analysis
  • Too many paths. If you really need to analyze all of the 256 paths individually, rerun with max-paths=256
  • RecMII not computed since number of paths is unknown or > max_paths
  • Streams not analyzed since number of paths is unknown or > max_paths
Try to simplify control and/or increase the maximum number of paths per function/loop through the 'max-paths-nb' option. This loop has 256 execution paths. The presence of multiple execution paths is typically the main/first bottleneck. Try to simplify control inside loop: ideally, try to remove all conditional expressions, for example by (if applicable):
  • hoisting them (moving them outside the loop)
  • turning them into conditional moves, MIN or MAX
Ex: if (x<0) x=0 => x = max(0,x) (Fortran instrinsic procedure)
